2604.12069.title: Robust Explanations for User Trust in Enterprise NLP Systems
2604.12069.paper_summary: Robust explanations are increasingly required for user trust in enterprise NLP, yet pre-deployment validation is difficult in the common case of black-box deployment (API-only access) where representation-based explainers are infeasible and existing studies provide limited guidance on whether explanations remain stable under real user noise, especially when organizations migrate from encoder classifiers to decoder LLMs. To close this gap, we propose a unified black-box robustness evaluation framework for token-level explanations based on leave-one-out occlusion, and operationalize explanation robustness with top-token flip rate under realistic perturbations (swap, deletion, shuffling, and back-translation) at multiple severity levels. Using this protocol, we conduct a systematic cross-architecture comparison across three benchmark datasets and six models spanning encoder and decoder families (BERT, RoBERTa, Qwen 7B/14B, Llama 8B/70B; 64,800 cases). We find that decoder LLMs produce substantially more stable explanations than encoder baselines (73% lower flip rates on average), and that stability improves with model scale (44% gain from 7B to 70B). Finally, we relate robustness improvements to inference cost, yielding a practical cost-robustness tradeoff curve that supports model and explanation selection prior to deployment in compliance-sensitive applications.
2604.12069.paper_summary_zh: 穩健的解釋對於企業自然語言處理中的用戶信任越來越重要，然而在黑箱部署（僅限API訪問）的常見情況下，預部署驗證變得困難，這使得基於表示的解釋者不可行，且現有研究對於解釋在真實用戶噪聲下是否保持穩定提供的指導有限，特別是在組織從編碼器分類器遷移到解碼器大型語言模型時。為了填補這一空白，我們提出了一個基於逐一遮蔽的統一黑箱穩健性評估框架，用於令牌級解釋，並通過在多個嚴重程度下的現實擾動（交換、刪除、洗牌和回譯）下的頂部令牌翻轉率來操作化解釋穩健性。使用這一協議，我們在三個基準數據集和六個模型（涵蓋編碼器和解碼器系列：BERT、RoBERTa、Qwen 7B/14B、Llama 8B/70B；共64,800個案例）之間進行了系統的跨架構比較。我們發現解碼器大型語言模型產生的解釋比編碼器基準更加穩定（平均翻轉率低73%），並且隨著模型規模的增加，穩定性有所提高（從7B到70B增長44%）。最後，我們將穩健性改進與推理成本相關聯，產生了一個實用的成本-穩健性權衡曲線，支持在合規敏感的應用中進行模型和解釋的選擇。
2604.12069.author: Guilin Zhang et.al.
2604.12069.authors: Guilin Zhang, Kai Zhao, Jeffrey Friedman, Xu Chu, Amine Anoun, Jerry Ting
